content_sections.heading: Indications
content_sections.body: This guideline applies to all patients in cardiac arrest, including trauma cardiac arrest. Cardiac arrest is defined as the absence of signs of life (unresponsive, apnoeic, pulseless).

content_sections.heading: Clinical Management
content_sections.body:
Commence CPR immediately. Apply defibrillator pads as soon as possible. Monitor rhythm and follow the appropriate algorithm:

- VF/VT: Defibrillate immediately. Continue CPR while charging. Adrenaline 1 mg IV/IO after third shock and every 3-5 minutes thereafter. Amiodarone 300 mg IV/IO after third shock.

- PEA/Asystole: Commence CPR immediately. Give Adrenaline 1 mg IV/IO as soon as IV access is achieved and every 3-5 minutes thereafter.

Reversible causes should be considered and treated where possible (4 Hs and 4 Ts).

content_sections.heading: Pharmacology
content_sections.body:
Adrenaline is a sympathomimetic amine that acts on alpha, beta-1, and beta-2 adrenergic receptors. In cardiac arrest, it increases coronary and cerebral perfusion pressure by peripheral vasoconstriction.

Amiodarone is a class III antiarrhythmic agent that prolongs the action potential duration and refractory period in cardiac tissue. It is used for shock-refractory VF/VT.
